atoms.source_snippet:
pub trait PoolBuilder<Node: FullNodeTypes>: Send {
    /// The transaction pool to build.
    type Pool: TransactionPool<Transaction: PoolTransaction<Consensus = TxTy<Node::Types>>>
        + reth_transaction_pool::TransactionPoolExt
        + Unpin
        + 'static;
...
Pool: TransactionPool<Transaction: PoolTransaction<Consensus = TxTy<Node::Types>>>
    + reth_transaction_pool::TransactionPoolExt
    + Unpin
    + 'static,
atoms.semantic_claim: The old pool-builder API requires every constructed pool to implement `TransactionPoolExt`, making extended transaction-pool operations available to downstream builder/EVM/block-verification code.

destinations.evidence: The merge notes explicitly say not to carry forward the old build_evm_with_pool shim. They direct custom execution to the current ExecutorBuilder shape and say async/scheduled txpool behavior should map to PoolBuilder<Node, Evm>, transaction validation, or pool maintenance because the pool now receives the builder-created EVM config directly.

Current component construction builds evm_config with executor_builder.build_evm(context), then calls pool_builder.build_pool(context, evm_config.clone()), then wires network and payload builder with the pool and EVM config. This is the intended v2 ownership direction: EVM config into pool, not pool into EVM construction.

The current PoolBuilder is generic over Evm and build_pool receives evm_config. Concrete maintenance helpers still require TransactionPoolExt and spawn maintain_transaction_pool_future on the provider canonical_state_stream.

When the pool receives canonical-state changes, it updates the normal txpool, processes canonical async commitments into the async lifecycle pool, processes canonical async settlements/deliveries, and cleans up expired async entries. This proves the old pool-aware async lifecycle work moved to pool maintenance rather than EVM construction.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Confirmed after mechanical review: the old trait-level TransactionPoolExt guarantee was part of the old build_evm_with_pool/pool-aware verifier API shape and is intentionally not preserved at the PoolBuilder associated-type boundary. Current concrete pool maintenance helpers still require TransactionPoolExt where extended methods are used. The old broad trait guarantee is therefore an intentional API-shape difference; the real remaining FSM non-equivalence is synchronous blocking-handler registration, tracked in files 194 and 197.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Focus tests on the concrete TransactionPoolExt users: pool maintenance should process canonical async commitments/settlements and sender locks as expected.
atoms.notes: Reviewed 2026-05-11: no unresolved proof remains. Trait-level bound is intentionally absent; concrete helper bounds preserve current extended-pool usage.

summary: Reviewed and downgraded: the old PoolBuilder-wide TransactionPoolExt guarantee is intentionally absent with the old build_evm_with_pool architecture. Current helpers require TransactionPoolExt at concrete maintenance boundaries where needed.
notes: Reviewed 2026-05-11 against current port code and MERGE_NOTES.
Current PoolBuilder<Node, Evm> does not require TransactionPoolExt on every associated pool type.
Concrete maintenance helpers such as spawn_pool_maintenance_task still require TransactionPoolExt.
Pool::on_canonical_state_change processes async lifecycle state once maintenance receives canonical updates.
Residual synchronous ordering gap is tracked in files 194 and 197.
